keyChangesVsYesterday: DDR5 spot 6th consecutive rise but DECELERATION — $40.767 (+0.17%, vs previous +0.83%)
Framework independent validation: DDR5 stable, SSD continue to soar
Best Buy D5-P5336 61.44TB dropped $1,310 to $26,207 — consumer-side pullback
Micron exits Crucial consumer brand — consumer DRAM supply further contraction
Samsung PM1743 3.84TB E3.S $1,200 vs U.3 $2,999 — form factor split 2.5x
Compuram Kioxia CM7-R 7.68TB $4,290 — new European channel reference

DDR5-6400.reason: 16-day anchor, 6-day spot rise direction confirmed, DRAM Q1 +110-115%, Micron Crucial exit tightens consumer supply, LTA locks supply advantage. Deceleration in spot doesn't reverse direction.

DDR5-5600.reason: 6-day spot rise but acceleration falsified as one-day pulse; $41 may form resistance; Framework 'fairly stable' validates platform phase. Annual direction clear (Citi +82%) but near-term pace may shift.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: Brand split Day 11 structural; entire chain sold out 13 days; Framework SSD continuing upward; Samsung Japan +300%. 6500 ION EOL Day 8. Tracking lag 56-122%. HIGHEST URGENCY category.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: 30.72TB Day 8 price rise confirmed reliable. But 7.68TB 4.7x band, 3.84TB 2.5x form factor split — small/mid capacity signals chaotic. Large capacity direction clear but not applicable to all specs.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Mouser premium upper bound Day 5 sustained + NAND Q2 +70-75%. But Best Buy -4.8% and SS -$1,000 suggest some channel elasticity. Upward direction intact but slope may be moderating. Approaching buy_now threshold.
